shapes: layer stack with a shared base

prose.rule: Explanatory plates are hand-authored SVG. Palette from the structured block and nothing else. Type is system sans, never a webfont, so the file renders standalone. Minimum 2px strokes with round linecaps, so it survives being scaled down. Theme-aware via a `prefers-color-scheme` block INSIDE the svg, because an `` embed puts the page's CSS out of reach. `role="img"` plus a `` element, and the surrounding alt text still carries the full description in prose. No gradients, no shadows, no 3D, no isometric: flat fills and honest lines, matching the ink family it sits beside.
prose.whyNotGenerated: Four reasons, each of which has cost a real render. An image model silently drops and misspells letters, and a diagram whose whole value is that its labels are right cannot survive that. Raster type blurs on retina and in print. A diagram is edited far more often than it is drawn, and a generated one must be re-rolled rather than corrected. And only vector can follow the reader's light or dark theme.
prose.restraint: One structure per plate. If it needs a legend longer than two lines, it is two plates. The reader should get the shape in about three seconds and the detail on a second pass; a plate that requires study has failed at the only job it had.
prose.source: Written 2026-07-22 alongside one-job-per-plate, from the three diagrams built for the /the-argument essay: the rented-versus-yours split, the fifty-step trajectory with its silent failure marked, and the four altitudes over a shared Messages API base.
